meta.purpose: Terminology boundaries, traps, and self-checks for AI Animation Workflow.
meta.load_condition: Load with Core for any generation, review, or refactor task in this domain.

standard_terms.definition: A generation pattern where the agent produces a structured motion brief for review before writing any implementation code.
A documented list of context the agent inferred but was not explicitly given — visible to the human reviewer so they can correct wrong assumptions early.
An explicit checkpoint in the workflow where human approval is required before the agent proceeds to the next stage.
A summary of what changed between animation versions, including which criterion each change addresses.

banned_terms.why: 'Good' is untestable. Require the agent to state which specific criteria (attention, timing, density, performance) were improved and how.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

banned_terms.why: Generating multiple un-reviewed variants without a shared brief multiplies review burden. Generate one well-briefed version, review it, then iterate.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

banned_terms.why: Never embed assumptions silently in the output. State them explicitly in an assumption log before generating.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

misunderstandings.wrong: A good enough AI agent can generate production animation without human review.
misunderstandings.correct: Agents can generate correct, performant, and accessible animation — but taste requires human calibration. The agent implements; the human directs.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: Agents can generate correct, performant, and accessible animation — but taste requires human calibration. The agent implements; the human directs.
misunderstandings.why: Holding the wrong interpretation leads to poor judgment: Agents can generate correct, performant, and accessible animation — but taste requires human calibration. The agent implements; the human directs.

misunderstandings.wrong: Generating many animation variants at once is faster than iterating on one.
misunderstandings.correct: Reviewing 5 variants with unstated assumptions is slower than reviewing 1 variant with a clear brief and 2 targeted revisions. Volume hides direction problems.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: Reviewing 5 variants with unstated assumptions is slower than reviewing 1 variant with a clear brief and 2 targeted revisions. Volume hides direction problems.
misunderstandings.why: Holding the wrong interpretation leads to poor judgment: Reviewing 5 variants with unstated assumptions is slower than reviewing 1 variant with a clear brief and 2 targeted revisions. Volume hides direction problems.

self_check: Did the agent produce a structured brief with audience, attention path, and emotional tone before any code?
Are all agent assumptions about the context explicitly listed and reviewable?
Was the minimal viable animation reviewed by a human before expansion?
Does each revision state what changed and which quality criterion it addresses?
